title: Prayer is the soul’s sincere desire
firstLine: Prayer is the soul’s sincere desire,
publicDomainBasis: James Montgomery (1771–1854), James Walch (1837–1901). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Prayer is the soul’s sincere desire,
Uttered, or unexpressed;
The motion of a hidden fire
That trembles in the breast.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Prayer is the burden of a sigh,
The falling of a tear;
The upward glancing of an eye
When none but God is near.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Prayer is the simplest form of speech
That infant lips can try;
Prayer the sublimest strains that reach
The Majesty on high.

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 5
sections.text:
The saints in prayer appear as one,
In word, and deed, and mind;
While with the Father and the Son
Sweet fellowship they find.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
O Thou, by whom we come to God,
The Life, the Truth, the Way,
The path of prayer Thyself hast trod—
Lord, teach us how to pray.
